terms.definition: A framework for managing AI 'agents'—models capable of autonomous action—that optimizes how they interact with data products and GUIs. It uses vision-language models and self-evolving memory to allow AI to perform long-horizon workflows, correcting its own intermediate errors while navigating complex digital interfaces.
terms.analogy: In a high-end kitchen, traditional AI is the recipe book, but an Agentic system is the Executive Chef who not only reads the recipe but actually chops the vegetables, adjusts the heat when the pan gets too hot, and coordinates the sous-chefs to plate the meal.
terms.business_value: Shifts AI from a passive 'chatbot' to an active 'digital employee,' allowing organizations to automate multi-step administrative and technical tasks that previously required expensive human oversight.

terms.definition: The process of transferring complex logical 'thinking' patterns from a massive Large Reasoning Model (LRM) into a smaller, more efficient model. Techniques like HEAL use hindsight learning to ensure the smaller model learns the 'how' behind a solution, even when the teacher model initially fails on complex corner-cases.
terms.analogy: Think of this as a Michelin-starred chef writing a foolproof, step-by-step training manual for a fast-casual kitchen; it allows the less experienced staff to produce gourmet results without needing the expensive head chef present for every plate.
terms.business_value: Enables the deployment of high-intelligence logic on edge devices and private servers at a fraction of the hardware cost and latency, democratizing 'expert-level' AI across the enterprise.

terms.definition: A security and governance framework that establishes a 'chain of command' for AI models to resolve conflicting prompts. It ensures that system-level safety guidelines and developer constraints take precedence over user inputs, preventing 'jailbreaks' or unauthorized prompt injections.
terms.analogy: In a car, the passenger (the User) might tell the vehicle to 'speed up,' but the car’s internal Governor and Collision Avoidance system (the Instruction Hierarchy) refuses because the safety protocol overrides the passenger's request.
terms.business_value: Directly mitigates corporate liability and brand risk by ensuring AI agents remain within strictly defined policy guardrails, even when faced with sophisticated malicious attempts to bypass security.
